title: Manage Device Settings
summary: Change brightness, volume, storage, and notifications on your device.
learnerPromise: Change brightness, volume, storage, and notifications on your device.
whyItMatters: Your device works better when it is set up to fit your needs. Small adjustments can make the screen easier to read, save battery life, and reduce interruptions.
realLifeUses: Turning off notifications during a meeting or class.
Freeing up storage space so your device runs more smoothly.
Adjusting text size so it is easier to read.
youWillLearn: Open the main Settings app on your device.
Find and adjust display brightness and volume.
Check how much storage space is left.
Turn notifications on or off for specific apps.
miniChallenge: Change one setting you have not touched before, then change it back.
steps: Open the Settings app on your device.
Scroll through the menu and read the names of each section.
Pick one setting you want to change, such as brightness or sound.
Adjust it using the slider or toggle and notice what changes.
Change it back to its original position if you prefer the old setting.
completionCriteria: You can find and open your device's Settings on your own.
You adjusted at least one setting and saw the result.
commonProblems: There are too many options — start with Display or Sound, which are usually near the top.
You are not sure what a setting does — tap the small info icon or read the description below it.
You changed something by accident — most settings can be undone right away.
tips: Make small changes one at a time so you know what caused the effect.
If your device feels slow, check Storage or Battery sections first.
Write down the original value of a setting before you change it, just in case.
